Combined_Issue: ['Whether a holding company of an industrial group can hold a former senior manager to contractual and equitable duties arising from group-level employment documentation and structural arrangements when his formal employer entities are operating subsidiaries', "Whether a senior manager who transitions from a multinational's switchgear business to a new joint venture in the same industry contravened his contractual and equitable management obligations by engaging in preparatory acts and assistance for the new venture while still employed"]
Combined_Rule: ['A parent or holding entity seeking to rely on contractual or equitable obligations must show that the individual was its employee or officer, or that the relevant terms were clearly incorporated so as to extend duties to that entity, and courts construe ambiguous employment instruments contra proferentem against the drafting employer', "An employee in a senior management position must act in good faith towards his employer, avoid conflicts between his own future business plans and his employer's interests, disclose material information about emerging competitive threats, and refrain from using his position or working time to advance a competing enterprise before his employment ends, although he may plan to compete after departure if he does not misuse confidential information or solicit business during employment"]
Combined_Application: ["The holding company relied on employment letters it had issued and on the Employee Handbook to contend that group-wide obligations extended to it, while the former manager emphasised that his contractual employer was the operating subsidiary and that the handbook and later contracts did not expressly impose duties in favour of the holding entity. The parties disputed whether the structural role of the holding company and the manager's seniority sufficed to create direct obligations to the holding company absent clear contractual wording", "The defendant failed to disclose XIHARI's plans to develop and market a new generation of medium voltage products, facilitated the engagement of a former ABB expert as technical adviser to XIHARI and the new manufacturer, and used his time and knowledge to prepare Huadian's business plan before resigning, thereby placing his own future interests in conflict with his employer's. The defendant responded that his responsibilities were confined to low voltage products, that XIHARI as a Chinese research institute and Huadian as a Chinese manufacturer did not materially threaten the Singapore entities' markets, that he had no access to confidential medium voltage manufacturing information, and that his communications and planning fell within permissible preparation for post-employment activities"]

Combined_Issue: ['Whether a former president and director of a low voltage manufacturing company contravened his contractual and equitable management obligations by diverting a business opportunity and engaging in outside ventures while still owing duties to the company', "Whether a senior manager who transitions from a multinational's switchgear business to a new joint venture in the same industry contravened his contractual and equitable management obligations by engaging in preparatory acts and assistance for the new venture while still employed"]
Combined_Rule: ['Senior officers must avoid conflicts between their personal interests and those of their company, refrain from undisclosed participation in external businesses that could impair their loyalty or performance, and not exploit for personal gain opportunities that properly belong to their company or fall within its line of business', "An employee in a senior management position must act in good faith towards his employer, avoid conflicts between his own future business plans and his employer's interests, disclose material information about emerging competitive threats, and refrain from using his position or working time to advance a competing enterprise before his employment ends, although he may plan to compete after departure if he does not misuse confidential information or solicit business during employment"]
Combined_Application: ["The defendant orchestrated the Xiamen property project through Great Vision and Xiamen Fu Cheng while still in office, pointing to his role in sourcing the land opportunity, the RMB70,000 travel advance booked in his name, and his later assumption of directorship and shareholding. The defendant responded that the property development was outside the company's low voltage business, that he merely passed the opportunity to his brother and an investor without active involvement during his tenure, and that any accounting entry in his name was an error relating to others' travel", "The defendant failed to disclose XIHARI's plans to develop and market a new generation of medium voltage products, facilitated the engagement of a former ABB expert as technical adviser to XIHARI and the new manufacturer, and used his time and knowledge to prepare Huadian's business plan before resigning, thereby placing his own future interests in conflict with his employer's. The defendant responded that his responsibilities were confined to low voltage products, that XIHARI as a Chinese research institute and Huadian as a Chinese manufacturer did not materially threaten the Singapore entities' markets, that he had no access to confidential medium voltage manufacturing information, and that his communications and planning fell within permissible preparation for post-employment activities"]

Combined_Issue: ['Whether a senior manager of a switchgear business contravened his contractual and equitable management obligations by assisting a research institute and a new joint venture to develop and commercialise competing medium voltage products while still employed', "Whether a senior manager who transitions from a multinational's switchgear business to a new joint venture in the same industry contravened his contractual and equitable management obligations by engaging in preparatory acts and assistance for the new venture while still employed"]
Combined_Rule: ["Senior managers who occupy positions of trust must act in the best interests of their employer, avoid conflicts between their own future plans and their employer's business, disclose material information about emerging competitive threats, and refrain from using their time and position to advance a competing enterprise before their employment ends", "An employee in a senior management position must act in good faith towards his employer, avoid conflicts between his own future business plans and his employer's interests, disclose material information about emerging competitive threats, and refrain from using his position or working time to advance a competing enterprise before his employment ends, although he may plan to compete after departure if he does not misuse confidential information or solicit business during employment"]
Combined_Application: ["The defendant failed to disclose XIHARI's plans to develop and market a new generation of medium voltage products, actively facilitated the engagement of a former ABB expert as technical adviser to XIHARI and the new manufacturer, and used his time and knowledge to prepare Huadian's business plan before resigning. The defendant responded that his role was limited to communications for a Chinese research institute that did not directly compete in the company's markets, that he had no access to confidential medium voltage manufacturing information, and that his substantive responsibilities were confined to low voltage products in Asia South", "The defendant failed to disclose XIHARI's plans to develop and market a new generation of medium voltage products, facilitated the engagement of a former ABB expert as technical adviser to XIHARI and the new manufacturer, and used his time and knowledge to prepare Huadian's business plan before resigning, thereby placing his own future interests in conflict with his employer's. The defendant responded that his responsibilities were confined to low voltage products, that XIHARI as a Chinese research institute and Huadian as a Chinese manufacturer did not materially threaten the Singapore entities' markets, that he had no access to confidential medium voltage manufacturing information, and that his communications and planning fell within permissible preparation for post-employment activities"]

Combined_Issue: ['Whether a company, acting through a shareholder in a derivative capacity, can hold a former managing director legally responsible for contravening director and management obligations by diverting business opportunities and funds away from the company in circumstances where the Chairman was said to have directed or approved the transactions.', 'Whether a former managing director who carried out transactions said to divert corporate business and funds can avoid being held legally responsible to the company where she states that she was merely following the instructions or understanding of the Chairman who was treated as the controlling mind of the company.']
Combined_Rule: ['A director and managing director must act in good faith in the interests of the company, avoid unauthorised diversion of corporate opportunities or assets, and may only rely on instructions or approvals from those controlling the company where such directions are within their authority and do not conflict with the company’s interests or applicable law.', 'A managing director owes independent duties to act in the company’s interests and cannot rely on directions from a dominant shareholder or Chairman to justify conduct that improperly diverts corporate opportunities or assets, unless such directions amount to valid corporate approval consistent with law and the company’s interests.']
Combined_Application: ['The plaintiff, acting for the company, stated that specific business opportunities and funds were diverted away from the company through transactions carried out by the managing director without proper corporate approval and contrary to the company’s interests. The managing director responded that she acted in accordance with oral instructions or an understanding with the Chairman, who was said to be the controlling mind of the company, and that the opportunities were not in truth the company’s business.', 'Linda stated that she received and disbursed funds and business opportunities on behalf of Peter Fong’s friends and associates as a favour, in reliance on his specific and standing instructions and on his status as majority shareholder and Chairman. The company, acting through the derivative plaintiff, contended that Peter Fong either did not authorise the impugned transactions or lacked authority to authorise diversions that harmed the company, so that Linda remained independently responsible for her management decisions.']

Combined_Issue: ['Whether a company, acting through a shareholder in a derivative capacity, can hold a former managing director legally responsible for contravening director and management obligations by diverting business opportunities and funds away from the company in circumstances where the Chairman was said to have directed or approved the transactions.', 'Whether a managing director can be held legally responsible for post‑Chairman‑death diversions of business and assets where she maintains that her conduct was consistent with earlier instructions or understandings from the former Chairman and that she believed such conduct remained authorised.']
Combined_Rule: ['A director and managing director must act in good faith in the interests of the company, avoid unauthorised diversion of corporate opportunities or assets, and may only rely on instructions or approvals from those controlling the company where such directions are within their authority and do not conflict with the company’s interests or applicable law.', 'A managing director’s obligations to act in the company’s interests and to avoid improper diversion of business or assets continue regardless of changes in shareholding or the death of a dominant figure, and reliance on historic instructions or understandings cannot justify later conduct that conflicts with the company’s interests or governance structure.']
Combined_Application: ['The plaintiff, acting for the company, stated that specific business opportunities and funds were diverted away from the company through transactions carried out by the managing director without proper corporate approval and contrary to the company’s interests. The managing director responded that she acted in accordance with oral instructions or an understanding with the Chairman, who was said to be the controlling mind of the company, and that the opportunities were not in truth the company’s business.', 'The Receivers and Managers stated that after Peter Fong’s death, Linda and others diverted business away from the company and failed to comply with their obligations, causing loss to the company. Linda responded that her actions were in line with a prior pattern of instructions and understandings with Peter Fong and that she believed she remained authorised to manage funds and opportunities in that manner, while the Receivers and Managers argued that any such historic understanding could not validate the later diversions.']

Combined_Issue: ['Whether a company under the control of Receivers and Managers can hold a former managing director and associated individuals legally responsible for a coordinated diversion of business and for contravening director and management obligations in respect of transactions occurring after the death of the former Chairman.', 'Whether a former managing director who carried out transactions said to divert corporate business and funds can avoid being held legally responsible to the company where she states that she was merely following the instructions or understanding of the Chairman who was treated as the controlling mind of the company.']
Combined_Rule: ['Persons who direct or participate in the management of a company must not engage in concerted conduct that diverts the company’s business or assets for their own benefit or that of others, and a managing director remains subject to independent duties to act in the company’s interests even where prior instructions from a former Chairman are relied upon.', 'A managing director owes independent duties to act in the company’s interests and cannot rely on directions from a dominant shareholder or Chairman to justify conduct that improperly diverts corporate opportunities or assets, unless such directions amount to valid corporate approval consistent with law and the company’s interests.']
Combined_Application: ['The Receivers and Managers stated that after Peter Fong’s death, Linda and multiple co‑defendants diverted business opportunities and assets away from the company through coordinated arrangements that harmed the company. Linda responded that her conduct in the later period was consistent with earlier instructions or understandings with Peter Fong and that she believed she was authorised to act as she did, while the Receivers and Managers maintained that such reliance could not justify the diversions or relieve her of her independent obligations.', 'Linda stated that she received and disbursed funds and business opportunities on behalf of Peter Fong’s friends and associates as a favour, in reliance on his specific and standing instructions and on his status as majority shareholder and Chairman. The company, acting through the derivative plaintiff, contended that Peter Fong either did not authorise the impugned transactions or lacked authority to authorise diversions that harmed the company, so that Linda remained independently responsible for her management decisions.']

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Oon Swee Gek, a prominent Singaporean chef, incorporated the company to commercialize her culinary brand.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'She served as the Culinary Curator, Chef, and a founding director of the company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': "She executed a shareholders' agreement that diluted her family's stake in exchange for corporate investment."}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-12-01', 'Text': "Following the investment, her personal shareholding was reduced to 20% of the company's total shares."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-21', 'Text': 'She signed a revised set of binding terms transferring further corporate control away from her family due to a stated fear of corporate insolvency.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'The company operates a prominent chain of food and beverage outlets in Singapore.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'BOARD_ACTION', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': 'The company issued new shares to an external investment group, resulting in a 50-50 split in shareholding ownership.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-01', 'Text': 'The company experienced significant cash flow shortages and required capital injections to pay suppliers and staff.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': "The company's constitution and governance structures were formally altered following the execution of a new shareholders' agreement."}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether a founding shareholder is entitled to statutory relief for minority oppression if corporate restructuring agreements were extracted through economic duress.', 'Whether a corporate entity should be subject to a buyout order when its shareholders are deadlocked due to oppressive conduct by one faction.']
Combined_Rule: ['A shareholder may petition the court for a buyout or to set aside agreements if another shareholder leverages illegitimate commercial pressure and undue influence to commandeer the company.', "When a company's affairs are conducted in a manner that is commercially unfair to a shareholder, the court may intervene to regulate the company's affairs or order the purchase of shares to break the deadlock."]
Combined_Application: ['The Plaintiff stated that her co-investor manufactured a financial crisis to coerce her into surrendering control of her namesake business. The Defendants argued that the renegotiated terms were standard commercial rescue measures freely agreed to by all parties.', "The Claimants stated that the company was being improperly controlled by the investor faction. The Defendants argued that the company's affairs were being managed strictly according to the binding 2019 agreements."]

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Oon Swee Gek, a prominent Singaporean chef, incorporated the company to commercialize her culinary brand.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'She served as the Culinary Curator, Chef, and a founding director of the company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': "She executed a shareholders' agreement that diluted her family's stake in exchange for corporate investment."}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-12-01', 'Text': "Following the investment, her personal shareholding was reduced to 20% of the company's total shares."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-21', 'Text': 'She signed a revised set of binding terms transferring further corporate control away from her family due to a stated fear of corporate insolvency.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': 'Murjani Manoj Mohan negotiated a joint venture where his investment vehicle acquired a 50% stake in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-08-16', 'Text': 'He instructed the existing CEO to cease managing the business operations and step down from his role.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2018-12-01', 'Text': "He communicated exaggerated claims regarding the CEO's conduct to isolate him from the other founding members."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-21', 'Text': 'He threatened to withhold critical financial support for the company unless new shareholder terms were immediately agreed upon.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': "He procured the execution of a revised shareholders' agreement that shifted corporate control in favor of his investment vehicle."}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether a founding shareholder is entitled to statutory relief for minority oppression if corporate restructuring agreements were extracted through economic duress.', 'Whether a director is legally responsible for minority oppression after utilizing economic duress to force the execution of revised corporate agreements.']
Combined_Rule: ['A shareholder may petition the court for a buyout or to set aside agreements if another shareholder leverages illegitimate commercial pressure and undue influence to commandeer the company.', 'Corporate agreements may be set aside, and oppression remedies triggered, if a party leverages illegitimate economic pressure and undue influence to seize control of a company.']
Combined_Application: ['The Plaintiff stated that her co-investor manufactured a financial crisis to coerce her into surrendering control of her namesake business. The Defendants argued that the renegotiated terms were standard commercial rescue measures freely agreed to by all parties.', 'The Claimants stated that the director manufactured a corporate crisis and isolated a key family member to force them into signing highly unfavorable terms. The Defendant argued that the 2019 agreements were standard commercial renegotiations necessary to save the business.']

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Oon Swee Gek, a prominent Singaporean chef, incorporated the company to commercialize her culinary brand.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'She served as the Culinary Curator, Chef, and a founding director of the company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': "She executed a shareholders' agreement that diluted her family's stake in exchange for corporate investment."}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-12-01', 'Text': "Following the investment, her personal shareholding was reduced to 20% of the company's total shares."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-21', 'Text': 'She signed a revised set of binding terms transferring further corporate control away from her family due to a stated fear of corporate insolvency.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2014-01-01', 'Text': 'Group MMM Pte Ltd is an investment vehicle entirely owned and controlled by Murjani Manoj Mohan.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': 'The corporate vehicle executed an agreement to acquire 50% of the shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-01', 'Text': 'The vehicle provided various short-term financial advances to the company to cover operational shortfalls.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': 'Under the disputed 2019 agreements, the vehicle was granted enhanced veto rights and a more favorable debt repayment structure.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether a founding shareholder is entitled to statutory relief for minority oppression if corporate restructuring agreements were extracted through economic duress.', 'Whether an investment vehicle is legally responsible for oppressive corporate maneuvers orchestrated by its sole owner and director.']
Combined_Rule: ['A shareholder may petition the court for a buyout or to set aside agreements if another shareholder leverages illegitimate commercial pressure and undue influence to commandeer the company.', 'A corporate shareholder can be held legally responsible for minority oppression if its directing mind and will engages in commercially unfair conduct to prejudice other shareholders.']
Combined_Application: ['The Plaintiff stated that her co-investor manufactured a financial crisis to coerce her into surrendering control of her namesake business. The Defendants argued that the renegotiated terms were standard commercial rescue measures freely agreed to by all parties.', 'The Claimants stated the investment vehicle was the primary beneficiary of the illegitimate pressure campaign. The Defendants stated the vehicle legally secured its rights through valid contract negotiations.']

Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore
Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Oon Swee Gek, a prominent Singaporean chef, incorporated the company to commercialize her culinary brand.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'She served as the Culinary Curator, Chef, and a founding director of the company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': "She executed a shareholders' agreement that diluted her family's stake in exchange for corporate investment."}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-12-01', 'Text': "Following the investment, her personal shareholding was reduced to 20% of the company's total shares."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-21', 'Text': 'She signed a revised set of binding terms transferring further corporate control away from her family due to a stated fear of corporate insolvency.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd is a profitable operational company employing numerous staff across several restaurant outlets.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2022-01-01', 'Text': 'Despite the shareholder dispute, the company remained a going concern with ongoing commercial operations.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'BOARD_ACTION', 'Fact_Date': '2022-08-01', 'Text': 'The company became the subject of a winding-up application filed by its founding shareholders.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'DOCUMENT', 'Fact_Date': '2022-08-01', 'Text': "The company's constitutional documents required cooperative management which had ceased to function."}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether a founding shareholder is entitled to statutory relief for minority oppression if corporate restructuring agreements were extracted through economic duress.', 'Whether a financially viable operational company should be liquidated to resolve an unworkable shareholder deadlock.']
Combined_Rule: ['A shareholder may petition the court for a buyout or to set aside agreements if another shareholder leverages illegitimate commercial pressure and undue influence to commandeer the company.', 'Courts view winding up as a remedy of last resort and will generally prefer to order a share buyout if the company remains a viable, profitable enterprise.']
Combined_Application: ['The Plaintiff stated that her co-investor manufactured a financial crisis to coerce her into surrendering control of her namesake business. The Defendants argued that the renegotiated terms were standard commercial rescue measures freely agreed to by all parties.', 'The Claimants stated the company could no longer function properly due to the toxic boardroom environment. The opposing shareholders argued that liquidating a profitable brand would destroy value unnecessarily.']

Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore
Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Tay Su-Lyn served as a founding director and held a 15% shareholding in the company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-09-01', 'Text': 'She assumed primary responsibility for corporate human resources and marketing during a period of management transition.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2018-11-20', 'Text': 'She attended several meetings with the majority investor where the potential insolvency of the company was discussed.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': "She executed the revised 2019 Shareholders' Agreement."}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'The company operates a prominent chain of food and beverage outlets in Singapore.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'BOARD_ACTION', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': 'The company issued new shares to an external investment group, resulting in a 50-50 split in shareholding ownership.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-01', 'Text': 'The company experienced significant cash flow shortages and required capital injections to pay suppliers and staff.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': "The company's constitution and governance structures were formally altered following the execution of a new shareholders' agreement."}]]
Combined_Issue: ["Whether a director's consent to a corporate restructuring is legally valid when obtained through the threat of withholding necessary operational capital.", 'Whether a corporate entity should be subject to a buyout order when its shareholders are deadlocked due to oppressive conduct by one faction.']
Combined_Rule: ['Consent to a commercial agreement is vitiated if it is obtained through illegitimate pressure that leaves the consenting party with no reasonable practical alternative.', "When a company's affairs are conducted in a manner that is commercially unfair to a shareholder, the court may intervene to regulate the company's affairs or order the purchase of shares to break the deadlock."]
Combined_Application: ['The Plaintiff stated she was forced to sign the new agreements to prevent the immediate collapse of the family business. The Defendants argued she was an experienced businesswoman who made a calculated commercial decision.', "The Claimants stated that the company was being improperly controlled by the investor faction. The Defendants argued that the company's affairs were being managed strictly according to the binding 2019 agreements."]

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Tay Su-Lyn served as a founding director and held a 15% shareholding in the company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-09-01', 'Text': 'She assumed primary responsibility for corporate human resources and marketing during a period of management transition.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2018-11-20', 'Text': 'She attended several meetings with the majority investor where the potential insolvency of the company was discussed.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': "She executed the revised 2019 Shareholders' Agreement."}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': 'Murjani Manoj Mohan negotiated a joint venture where his investment vehicle acquired a 50% stake in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-08-16', 'Text': 'He instructed the existing CEO to cease managing the business operations and step down from his role.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2018-12-01', 'Text': "He communicated exaggerated claims regarding the CEO's conduct to isolate him from the other founding members."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-21', 'Text': 'He threatened to withhold critical financial support for the company unless new shareholder terms were immediately agreed upon.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': "He procured the execution of a revised shareholders' agreement that shifted corporate control in favor of his investment vehicle."}]]
Combined_Issue: ["Whether a director's consent to a corporate restructuring is legally valid when obtained through the threat of withholding necessary operational capital.", 'Whether a director is legally responsible for minority oppression after utilizing economic duress to force the execution of revised corporate agreements.']
Combined_Rule: ['Consent to a commercial agreement is vitiated if it is obtained through illegitimate pressure that leaves the consenting party with no reasonable practical alternative.', 'Corporate agreements may be set aside, and oppression remedies triggered, if a party leverages illegitimate economic pressure and undue influence to seize control of a company.']
Combined_Application: ['The Plaintiff stated she was forced to sign the new agreements to prevent the immediate collapse of the family business. The Defendants argued she was an experienced businesswoman who made a calculated commercial decision.', 'The Claimants stated that the director manufactured a corporate crisis and isolated a key family member to force them into signing highly unfavorable terms. The Defendant argued that the 2019 agreements were standard commercial renegotiations necessary to save the business.']

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Tay Yiming served as the Chief Executive Officer of the company and held a 15% shareholding.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-08-16', 'Text': 'He stepped down from his role as CEO and ceased managing day-to-day operations following instructions from the majority investor.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2018-09-01', 'Text': 'He was isolated from ongoing corporate management discussions and financial negotiations.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': "He signed the 2019 Shareholders' Agreement which drastically reduced his influence over the company."}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'The company operates a prominent chain of food and beverage outlets in Singapore.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'BOARD_ACTION', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': 'The company issued new shares to an external investment group, resulting in a 50-50 split in shareholding ownership.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-01', 'Text': 'The company experienced significant cash flow shortages and required capital injections to pay suppliers and staff.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': "The company's constitution and governance structures were formally altered following the execution of a new shareholders' agreement."}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether the systematic isolation and removal of a CEO by a co-investor constitutes actionable minority oppression.', 'Whether a corporate entity should be subject to a buyout order when its shareholders are deadlocked due to oppressive conduct by one faction.']
Combined_Rule: ['A shareholder may establish oppression if they are unfairly excluded from the management of a company in contravention of an underlying mutual understanding of shared control.', "When a company's affairs are conducted in a manner that is commercially unfair to a shareholder, the court may intervene to regulate the company's affairs or order the purchase of shares to break the deadlock."]

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Oon Swee Gek is a founding shareholder holding a 20% stake in the food and beverage company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2021-01-01', 'Text': 'Following the breakdown of the 2019 agreements, trust completely eroded between the founding family and the majority investor.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'BOARD_ACTION', 'Fact_Date': '2022-01-01', 'Text': 'The board of directors reached a state of complete deadlock regarding the strategic direction and management of the company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2022-08-01', 'Text': 'She filed an alternative application to wind up the company on just and equitable grounds due to the management deadlock.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2014-01-01', 'Text': 'Group MMM Pte Ltd is an investment vehicle entirely owned and controlled by Murjani Manoj Mohan.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-11-13', 'Text': 'The corporate vehicle executed an agreement to acquire 50% of the shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-01', 'Text': 'The vehicle provided various short-term financial advances to the company to cover operational shortfalls.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-02-26', 'Text': 'Under the disputed 2019 agreements, the vehicle was granted enhanced veto rights and a more favorable debt repayment structure.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether a company should be ordered to wind up on just and equitable grounds due to an irretrievable breakdown of trust between its shareholders.', 'Whether an investment vehicle is legally responsible for oppressive corporate maneuvers orchestrated by its sole owner and director.']
Combined_Rule: ['A court may order the winding up of a company if there is a complete deadlock in management or a fundamental breakdown of the mutual trust required to operate the business.', 'A corporate shareholder can be held legally responsible for minority oppression if its directing mind and will engages in commercially unfair conduct to prejudice other shareholders.']
Combined_Application: ['The Plaintiff stated that the business relationship was irreparably destroyed, necessitating a winding up if a buyout was not feasible. The Defendants argued the company was financially viable and should not be destroyed.', 'The Claimants stated the investment vehicle was the primary beneficiary of the illegitimate pressure campaign. The Defendants stated the vehicle legally secured its rights through valid contract negotiations.']

Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore
Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Oon Swee Gek is a founding shareholder holding a 20% stake in the food and beverage company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2021-01-01', 'Text': 'Following the breakdown of the 2019 agreements, trust completely eroded between the founding family and the majority investor.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'BOARD_ACTION', 'Fact_Date': '2022-01-01', 'Text': 'The board of directors reached a state of complete deadlock regarding the strategic direction and management of the company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2022-08-01', 'Text': 'She filed an alternative application to wind up the company on just and equitable grounds due to the management deadlock.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2012-03-26', 'Text': 'Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd is a profitable operational company employing numerous staff across several restaurant outlets.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2022-01-01', 'Text': 'Despite the shareholder dispute, the company remained a going concern with ongoing commercial operations.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'BOARD_ACTION', 'Fact_Date': '2022-08-01', 'Text': 'The company became the subject of a winding-up application filed by its founding shareholders.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'DOCUMENT', 'Fact_Date': '2022-08-01', 'Text': "The company's constitutional documents required cooperative management which had ceased to function."}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether a company should be ordered to wind up on just and equitable grounds due to an irretrievable breakdown of trust between its shareholders.', 'Whether a financially viable operational company should be liquidated to resolve an unworkable shareholder deadlock.']
Combined_Rule: ['A court may order the winding up of a company if there is a complete deadlock in management or a fundamental breakdown of the mutual trust required to operate the business.', 'Courts view winding up as a remedy of last resort and will generally prefer to order a share buyout if the company remains a viable, profitable enterprise.']
Combined_Application: ['The Plaintiff stated that the business relationship was irreparably destroyed, necessitating a winding up if a buyout was not feasible. Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2016-11-23', 'Text': 'Pioneer Energy Holdings Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-04-13', 'Text': 'Mr Zheng and Mr Xu entered into a Cooperation Agreement for the sale of 50% of Pioneer shares.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-07-03', 'Text': 'Pioneer took out two loans from UOB: an SME Working Capital Loan of $259,000 and a Bizmoney Loan of $100,000.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2018-05-28', 'Text': 'Mr Xu was appointed as a director of Pioneer.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2016-11-23', 'Text': 'Zhu Yimin was appointed as a director of Pioneer Energy Holdings Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2019-09-25', 'Text': 'Zhu Yimin resigned as a director of Pioneer Energy Holdings Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2019-10-11', 'Text': 'Zhu Yimin communicated with Xu Jinsong about the control and operations of Pioneer.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-12-22', 'Text': 'Zhu Yimin was involved in discussions regarding the Withdrawal Agreement between Xu Jinsong and Mr Zheng.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether a company can hold a former director legally responsible for financial discrepancies and unauthorized transactions during her tenure.', 'Whether a former director can be held legally responsible for stated unauthorized transactions during her tenure as a nominee director.']
Combined_Rule: ['A director must act in good faith and in the best interests of the company, avoiding conflicts of interest and unauthorized transactions.', 'A nominee director is expected to fulfill statutory obligations but is not typically involved in executive decision-making unless explicitly authorized.']
Combined_Application: ["The Plaintiffs stated that Ms Zhu was involved in unauthorized financial transactions and failed to act in the company's best interest. Ms Zhu argued she was only a nominee director with no executive role.", 'The Defendant argued she was a nominee director with no executive powers. The Plaintiffs stated she was involved in unauthorized transactions.']

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2020-01-01', 'Text': "Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd is a company incorporated in the People's Republic of China."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'Shida entered into a purchasing agreement with YZY for the supply of apples.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-05-08', 'Text': 'Shida supplied apples with a total value of US$2,077,952.80 to NTUC Fairprice.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2019-01-01', 'Text': 'YZY stopped making payments to Shida.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'DOCUMENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'Purchasing agreement signed between Shida and YZY.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-16', 'Text': 'Shida issued invoices to YZY for the supply of apples.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '1995-01-01', 'Text': 'Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd is a company incorporated in Singapore.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2019-01-01', 'Text': 'Hou Chao was a director and majority shareholder of YZY until 2019.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2011-01-01', 'Text': "NTUC Fairprice began making payments directly to YZY's OCBC bank account."}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2019-01-01', 'Text': 'YZY communicated to Shida that payments would be stopped.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-01-01', 'Text': 'YZY paid Shida US$8,829,148.20 for apples supplied.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'YZY stated entered into a purchasing agreement with Shida for apple supply.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether Shida contracted with YZY or YY for the supply of apples to NTUC Fairprice.', 'Whether YZY is legally responsible for the payment of apples supplied by Shida.']
Combined_Rule: ['A contract is formed when there is mutual consent and agreement on the terms between the parties.', 'A company is bound by the terms of a contract if it is proven that the contract was entered into by an authorized representative.']
Combined_Application: ['Shida argued that it had a valid contract with YZY, supported by purchasing agreements and invoices. YZY contended that the contract was with YY, not YZY.', 'YZY argued that the contract was with YY and not YZY, and that payments were made on behalf of YY. Shida maintained that YZY was the contracting party.']

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2020-01-01', 'Text': "Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd is a company incorporated in the People's Republic of China."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'Shida entered into a purchasing agreement with YZY for the supply of apples.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-05-08', 'Text': 'Shida supplied apples with a total value of US$2,077,952.80 to NTUC Fairprice.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2019-01-01', 'Text': 'YZY stopped making payments to Shida.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'DOCUMENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'Purchasing agreement signed between Shida and YZY.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-16', 'Text': 'Shida issued invoices to YZY for the supply of apples.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'Shida entered into a purchasing agreement with YZY for the supply of apples.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-05-08', 'Text': 'Shida supplied apples with a total value of US$2,077,952.80 to NTUC Fairprice.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2019-01-01', 'Text': 'Shida communicated to YZY regarding outstanding payments.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-06-19', 'Text': "Shida's associate transferred RMB3,410,000 to Mr Hou."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-08-09', 'Text': "Shida's associate transferred RMB4,000,000 to Mr Hou's associate."}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether Shida contracted with YZY or YY for the supply of apples to NTUC Fairprice.', 'Whether Shida was involved in a conspiracy with Mr Hou to cause YZY to make excess payments.']
Combined_Rule: ['A contract is formed when there is mutual consent and agreement on the terms between the parties.', 'A party is legally responsible for conspiracy if it is proven that they acted in concert with another to cause harm through unlawful means.']

Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore
Plaintiff_Name: Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd
Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2020-01-01', 'Text': "Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd is a company incorporated in the People's Republic of China."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'Shida entered into a purchasing agreement with YZY for the supply of apples.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2019-05-08', 'Text': 'Shida supplied apples with a total value of US$2,077,952.80 to NTUC Fairprice.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2019-01-01', 'Text': 'YZY stopped making payments to Shida.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'DOCUMENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'Purchasing agreement signed between Shida and YZY.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-16', 'Text': 'Shida issued invoices to YZY for the supply of apples.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2013-05-18', 'Text': 'Hou Chao was appointed as a director of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd (YZY).'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2013-05-18', 'Text': 'Hou Chao acquired 60% of the share capital of YZY, becoming its majority shareholder.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-06-19', 'Text': 'Hou Chao received a transfer of RMB3,410,000 into his bank account from an individual named Mou.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-08-09', 'Text': 'A sum of RMB4,000,000 was transferred from Mou to an individual named Zhou, who later transferred RMB4,196,800 to Hou Chao.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'BOARD_ACTION', 'Fact_Date': '2019-09-02', 'Text': 'Hou Chao was formally removed from his position as a director of YZY following an extraordinary general meeting.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether Shida contracted with YZY or YY for the supply of apples to NTUC Fairprice.', 'Whether a former director contravened management obligations by orchestrating excess corporate payments to a supplier in exchange for personal financial kickbacks.']
Combined_Rule: ['A contract is formed when there is mutual consent and agreement on the terms between the parties.', 'A director must act honestly and use reasonable diligence in the discharge of their duties, and must not authorize corporate payments that lack genuine commercial justification in order to secure a personal benefit.']
Combined_Application: ['Shida argued that it had a valid contract with YZY, supported by purchasing agreements and invoices. YZY contended that the contract was with YY, not YZY.', "The Plaintiff by counterclaim stated that the director manipulated purchase prices and received kickbacks disguised as personal loans from the supplier's associates. The Defendant by counterclaim argued that the personal financial transfers were entirely unrelated to the company's apple purchasing business."]

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-01-01', 'Text': 'YZY claimed to have overpaid Shida by US$466,994.53.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'YZY stated that the purchasing agreement with Shida was not genuine.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2019-01-01', 'Text': 'YZY communicated its counterclaim against Shida for excess payments.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-06-19', 'Text': "YZY stated that Mr Hou received RMB3,410,000 from Shida's associate."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-08-09', 'Text': "YZY stated that RMB4,000,000 was transferred to Mr Hou's associate."}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '1995-01-01', 'Text': 'Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd is a company incorporated in Singapore.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2019-01-01', 'Text': 'Hou Chao was a director and majority shareholder of YZY until 2019.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2011-01-01', 'Text': "NTUC Fairprice began making payments directly to YZY's OCBC bank account."}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2019-01-01', 'Text': 'YZY communicated to Shida that payments would be stopped.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-01-01', 'Text': 'YZY paid Shida US$8,829,148.20 for apples supplied.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-10-04', 'Text': 'YZY stated entered into a purchasing agreement with Shida for apple supply.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether YZY made excess payments to Shida and if so, whether these were due to a conspiracy involving Mr Hou.', 'Whether YZY is legally responsible for the payment of apples supplied by Shida.']
Combined_Rule: ['A claim for excess payments requires proof of overpayment and a causal link to any stated misconduct.', 'A company is bound by the terms of a contract if it is proven that the contract was entered into by an authorized representative.']
Combined_Application: ['YZY argued that Mr Hou conspired with Shida to cause overpayments. Shida and Mr Hou denied any conspiracy or excess payments.', 'YZY argued that the contract was with YY and not YZY, and that payments were made on behalf of YY. Shida maintained that YZY was the contracting party.']

Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore
Plaintiff_Name: Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd
Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2014-02-20', 'Text': 'Tendcare was incorporated in Singapore by Mr Gong as Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2013-09-03', 'Text': 'HXG entered into a memorandum of understanding with BJTJ for the purpose of an IPO of Tendcare.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-06-09', 'Text': 'Tendcare received US$14,992,805.48 and US$5,007,187.57 from EFG Atlantis and Atlantis China respectively.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-02-06', 'Text': 'Tendcare received US$49,987,532.13 from Chow Tai Fook Nominees Ltd in respect of the Easom investment.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-09-16', 'Text': 'Tendcare received US$11,348,272.82 from OCA.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-09-10', 'Text': 'OCA agreed to provide a US$19,978,280 loan to Tendcare via a Convertible Note Subscription Agreement.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2014-02-20', 'Text': 'Mr Gong was appointed director of Tendcare and held the position at all material times.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-06-13', 'Text': 'Tendcare transferred US$5m to HXTJ shortly after receiving funds from EFG Atlantis and Atlantis China.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-02-06', 'Text': 'Tendcare transferred US$15m and US$5m to HXTJ after receiving funds from Chow Tai Fook Nominees Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-09-22', 'Text': 'Tendcare transferred US$4m to Tian Jian HK, which was then transferred to QHC.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-11-13', 'Text': 'Tendcare transferred US$3m to HXTJ after receiving funds from Chow Tai Fook Nominees Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-12-08', 'Text': 'Tendcare transferred US$4m to HXTJ.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether the business of Tendcare was carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose.', "Whether Mr Gong was knowingly a party to the carrying on of Tendcare's business with intent to defraud."]
Combined_Rule: ['A business is carried on with intent to defraud if there is dishonesty in the form of deception practised on an innocent party.', 'A person is knowingly a party to fraudulent trading if they authorize or cause transactions that misappropriate funds.']
Combined_Application: ['The plaintiffs argued that the Tendcare IPO was a ruse to raise funds that were misappropriated. The defendants claimed the funds were used for legitimate business purposes.', 'The plaintiffs showed that Mr Gong authorized transfers that were not used for the intended business purposes. Mr Gong did not provide a satisfactory explanation.']

Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore
Plaintiff_Name: Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd
Defendant_Name: Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd
Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2014-02-20', 'Text': 'Tendcare was incorporated in Singapore by Mr Gong as Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2013-09-03', 'Text': 'HXG entered into a memorandum of understanding with BJTJ for the purpose of an IPO of Tendcare.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-06-09', 'Text': 'Tendcare received US$14,992,805.48 and US$5,007,187.57 from EFG Atlantis and Atlantis China respectively.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-02-06', 'Text': 'Tendcare received US$49,987,532.13 from Chow Tai Fook Nominees Ltd in respect of the Easom investment.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-09-16', 'Text': 'Tendcare received US$11,348,272.82 from OCA.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-09-10', 'Text': 'OCA agreed to provide a US$19,978,280 loan to Tendcare via a Convertible Note Subscription Agreement.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2014-03-03', 'Text': "Tendcare allegedly entered into an 'Entrustment Agreement' with HXTJ."}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-02-06', 'Text': 'Tendcare transferred US$15m and US$5m to HXTJ.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-09-22', 'Text': 'Tendcare transferred US$4m to HXTJ via Tian Jian HK.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2015-11-13', 'Text': 'Tendcare transferred US$3m to HXTJ.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether the business of Tendcare was carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose.', 'Whether Tendcare is legally responsible to reimburse HXTJ for sums allegedly disbursed under the Entrustment Agreement.']
Combined_Rule: ['A business is carried on with intent to defraud if there is dishonesty in the form of deception practised on an innocent party.', 'A company is legally responsible for reimbursement if it is proven that funds were disbursed under a valid agreement for legitimate purposes.']
Combined_Application: ['The plaintiffs argued that the Tendcare IPO was a ruse to raise funds that were misappropriated. The defendants claimed the funds were used for legitimate business purposes.', 'Tendcare disputed the existence and terms of the Entrustment Agreement and the legitimacy of the disbursements.']

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2008-03-31', 'Text': 'Patrick John Wee Ewe Seng signed an employment contract with True Yoga Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-05-19', 'Text': 'CJ Group Pte Ltd transferred its shares in the plaintiffs to True Fitness Holdings (Singapore) Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-05-29', 'Text': 'Tongfang Kontafarma Holdings Limited acquired majority shareholding in True Group (Singapore).'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-06-10', 'Text': 'True Group (Malaysia) ceased operations.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-06-09', 'Text': 'True Group (Thailand) ceased operations.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2008-03-19', 'Text': 'Patrick John Wee Ewe Seng became the Group CEO of True Group.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2018-05-09', 'Text': 'Patrick John Wee Ewe Seng was terminated from his position as Group CEO.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-02-01', 'Text': 'Patrick John Wee Ewe Seng knew of financial difficulties in True Group (Malaysia) and allowed long-term memberships to be sold.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2017-11-30', 'Text': 'Patrick John Wee Ewe Seng caused S$3.25 million to be paid to his beneficially owned company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2017-03-01', 'Text': 'Patrick John Wee Ewe Seng communicated with potential investors about exiting the Malaysian and Thailand markets.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether the defendant was legally responsible for mismanaging the closures of True Group (Malaysia) and True Group (Thailand).', 'Whether the defendant was legally responsible for the financial mismanagement leading to the closures of True Group (Malaysia) and True Group (Thailand).']
Combined_Rule: ['A director must act in the best interests of the company and manage its affairs diligently.', 'A director is required to manage company affairs in a manner that serves the best interests of the company and its stakeholders.']

Combined_Issue: ['Whether a company can claim damages for contraventions of employment agreements and management obligations by a former director.', 'Whether a former director is legally responsible for contravening employment agreements and management obligations by engaging in competing business activities.']
Combined_Rule: ['A company may seek damages if it can prove that a director contravened employment agreements and management obligations, causing financial harm.', 'A director must not engage in activities that conflict with their obligations to the company unless explicitly permitted by agreement.']

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2001-12-01', 'Text': 'The consumer, a television actress, learned about the slimming pills through fellow actors and promotional materials.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2002-01-11', 'Text': 'She procured a supply of the slimming pills through a colleague rather than purchasing them directly from an authorized retail pharmacy.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2002-02-15', 'Text': 'She consumed the pills for several weeks to manage her weight.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2002-04-14', 'Text': 'She suffered severe liver failure requiring an emergency liver transplant after consuming the pills.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2001-03-01', 'Text': 'The company entered into an exclusive distributorship agreement to market and sell a brand of imported slimming pills.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2001-12-01', 'Text': 'The company launched an aggressive mass-media advertising campaign featuring celebrity endorsements claiming the product was 100% safe.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2001-12-01', 'Text': 'The company did not conduct any independent laboratory testing to verify the ingredients of the pills before distributing them.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'DOCUMENT', 'Fact_Date': '2001-11-01', 'Text': "The company relied entirely on the importer's assurances and the health authority's standard import documentation regarding product safety."}]]
Combined_Issue: ["Whether a consumer's failure to purchase a product from an authorized retailer breaks the chain of causation in a product liability claim against the distributor.", 'Whether a sole distributor is legally responsible in tort to consumers for failing to independently test a product that it aggressively advertises as completely safe.']
Combined_Rule: ["A consumer's method of procurement does not break the chain of causation if the distributor actively marketed the product as universally safe and the procurement method did not introduce new risks.", "A sole distributor who actively promotes a product and makes specific safety claims assumes a duty of care to consumers and cannot blindly rely on the importer's assurances without conducting independent verification."]
Combined_Application: ["The Plaintiff stated she relied on the distributor's heavy advertising campaigns assuring the product was 100% safe. The Defendant argued she assumed the risk by buying the pills through an unofficial channel.", 'The Defendant argued it was merely a distributor and was entitled to rely on the importer and regulatory authorities for safety checks. The Plaintiff stated that by acting as the sole promoter and expressly guaranteeing safety, the distributor assumed a primary duty to verify those claims.']

Coram: Chao Hick Tin JA; Woo Bih Li J; Yong Pung How CJ
Tribunal_Court: Court of Appeal
Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2001-12-01', 'Text': 'The consumer, a television actress, learned about the slimming pills through fellow actors and promotional materials.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2002-01-11', 'Text': 'She procured a supply of the slimming pills through a colleague rather than purchasing them directly from an authorized retail pharmacy.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2002-02-15', 'Text': 'She consumed the pills for several weeks to manage her weight.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2002-04-14', 'Text': 'She suffered severe liver failure requiring an emergency liver transplant after consuming the pills.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2001-01-01', 'Text': 'He was the controlling director and guiding mind of the company that imported the slimming pills.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2001-11-01', 'Text': 'He personally submitted import documentation to the health authorities omitting the existence of toxic ingredients in the pills.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2001-11-15', 'Text': 'He authorized the release of the pills to the sole distributor without establishing any system for batch testing or quality control.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2001-12-01', 'Text': 'He assured the distributor that the pills were entirely safe and compliant with local health regulations.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ["Whether a consumer's failure to purchase a product from an authorized retailer breaks the chain of causation in a product liability claim against the distributor.", 'Whether the controlling director of an importing company is personally liable in negligence for authorizing the distribution of a toxic product.']
Combined_Rule: ["A consumer's method of procurement does not break the chain of causation if the distributor actively marketed the product as universally safe and the procurement method did not introduce new risks.", "Directors are not automatically liable for a company's negligence, but personal liability attaches if the director was personally involved in directing or procuring the specific negligent acts."]
Combined_Application: ["The Plaintiff stated she relied on the distributor's heavy advertising campaigns assuring the product was 100% safe. The Defendant argued she assumed the risk by buying the pills through an unofficial channel.", 'The Defendant argued that any negligence was committed by the company, and he was shielded by corporate limited liability. The Plaintiff stated the Defendant was the sole decision-maker who actively drove the negligent importation process.']

Combined_Rule: ['A corporate director can be held personally liable for the torts of the company if they expressly directed, authorized, or procured the commission of the tortious act.', "A sole distributor who actively promotes a product and makes specific safety claims assumes a duty of care to consumers and cannot blindly rely on the importer's assurances without conducting independent verification."]

Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '1979-01-01', 'Text': 'TYC Investment Pte Ltd was established as a family holding company.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2012-06-11', 'Text': 'TYC entered into a Deed with HT and JC regarding management and payment approvals.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CHRONOLOGY', 'Fact_Date': '2013-09-04', 'Text': "An extraordinary general meeting was held to address JC's refusal to approve payments."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2012-01-01', 'Text': 'HT and JC agreed that both must approve payments by TYC.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2013-04-01', 'Text': 'KPMG submitted an invoice for $19,424.05 to TYC.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2013-09-11', 'Text': "HT informed TYC's shareholders of TSMP's appointment by email."}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '1979-01-01', 'Text': 'Jannie Chan Siew Lee was appointed as a permanent Governing Director of TYC.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CHRONOLOGY', 'Fact_Date': '2012-07-01', 'Text': 'JC refused to approve certain payments by TYC.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2012-06-11', 'Text': 'JC entered into a Deed with HT and TYC regarding management and payment approvals.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2013-09-11', 'Text': "JC informed HT and TSMP that she did not recognize TSMP's authority to act for TYC."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2013-09-04', 'Text': 'JC did not attend the extraordinary general meeting called by HT.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2013-04-01', 'Text': "JC refused to approve payment of KPMG's invoice for $19,424.05."}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether a company can enforce payment obligations against a director who refuses to approve payments under a mutual agreement.', 'Whether a director can be compelled to approve payments when there is a deadlock in management decisions.']

Combined_Rule: ['Directors must act in the best interest of the company and ensure that all transactions are conducted with transparency and proper authorization.', 'A director must ensure that all significant company transactions are approved by the board or shareholders to avoid contravening management obligations.']

The plaintiff stated that these actions were unauthorized.']

Tribunal_Court: High Court of Singapore
Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2004-01-01', 'Text': 'Zolton Techs Singapore Pte Ltd was set up by the third party.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CORPORATE_ROLE', 'Fact_Date': '2006-10-01', 'Text': 'Tan Chew Sim was appointed a director of Zolton Techs Singapore Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2008-01-01', 'Text': "Zolton Techs provided maintenance services to 7-Eleven's mashed potato machines."}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2009-01-01', 'Text': "7-Eleven's manager discussed with the defendant about supplying new mashed potato machines."}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2010-07-12', 'Text': 'A purchase order for 100 machines was sent by 7-Eleven to the defendant, addressed to Zolton Techs.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2010-09-01', 'Text': 'Mashed potato machines were supplied to 7-Eleven by SCM, a company controlled by the defendant.'}], [{'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2010-01-01', 'Text': "An oral agreement was made for the defendant to receive a 5% incentive bonus based on the plaintiff's revenue."}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2011-05-01', 'Text': 'The defendant claimed unpaid salary and bonuses from the plaintiff.'}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether the defendant was legally responsible for diverting a business opportunity from the plaintiff to SCM.', "Whether the company is legally responsible to pay the defendant's claimed salary and bonuses."]
Combined_Rule: ['A director must not contravene their management obligations by diverting corporate opportunities for personal gain without consent.', 'A company is bound to honor employment agreements unless validly contested.']
Combined_Application: ['The plaintiff stated that the defendant diverted the opportunity without consent. The defendant argued that the third party consented to the arrangement.', 'The plaintiff argued the defendant was not entitled to the claimed amounts due to stated misconduct. The defendant stated the claims were based on valid agreements.']

Tribunal_Court: High Court of Singapore
Combined_Facts: [[{'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2010-01-01', 'Text': "An oral agreement was made for the defendant to receive a 5% incentive bonus based on the plaintiff's revenue."}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2011-05-01', 'Text': 'The defendant claimed unpaid salary and bonuses from the plaintiff.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2011-04-04', 'Text': 'The defendant obtained an expedited personal protection order against the third party.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2011-05-09', 'Text': "The defendant's employment with the plaintiff was terminated."}], [{'Fact_Type': 'PARTY_INFO', 'Fact_Date': '2006-10-01', 'Text': 'Tan Chew Sim was appointed a director of Zolton Techs Singapore Pte Ltd.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'RELATIONSHIP', 'Fact_Date': '2006-10-01', 'Text': 'Tan Chew Sim married the third party, Chow Hoo Siong.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONTRACT_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2010-07-12', 'Text': 'A purchase order for 100 machines was sent by 7-Eleven to the defendant, addressed to Zolton Techs.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'CONDUCT', 'Fact_Date': '2010-09-01', 'Text': 'Mashed potato machines were supplied to 7-Eleven by SCM, a company controlled by the defendant.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'COMMUNICATION', 'Fact_Date': '2010-07-15', 'Text': 'Lim from 7-Eleven requested the defendant to arrange installation and demonstration of a mashed potato machine.'}, {'Fact_Type': 'FINANCIAL_EVENT', 'Fact_Date': '2011-04-01', 'Text': "The defendant incurred personal expenses on the plaintiff's corporate credit card."}]]
Combined_Issue: ['Whether the defendant is entitled to salary in lieu of notice and a 5% incentive bonus.', "Whether the defendant was legally responsible for personal expenses charged to the plaintiff's corporate credit card."]
Combined_Rule: ['An employee is entitled to salary in lieu of notice and agreed bonuses unless validly terminated for misconduct.', 'A director may incur personal expenses on corporate accounts only with explicit consent or ratification by the company.']
Combined_Application: ['The defendant stated she was entitled to these payments based on prior agreements. The plaintiff argued the defendant was not entitled due to stated misconduct.', 'The defendant stated that there was an understanding with the third party allowing such expenses. The plaintiff argued no such agreement existed.']
